title: Storefront Frontmatter v1
description: Optional YAML frontmatter block declaring how an internal markdown artifact projects to client-facing storefront surfaces. Per-artifact contract (not per-repo — see seed.yaml for that). Default behavior when absent: artifact remains internal, excluded from storefront builds. Substrate refuses to render orphans — every storefront-rendered artifact must trace bridge_to lineage to a real internal source. Consumed by the personalized-storefront-render skill and the `organvm storefront` CLI subcommand.

audiences.description: Audience targets for this artifact. Each entry projects one client-facing surface. If absent or only contains {id: internal}, artifact is excluded from storefront builds.

tldr.description: Single-sentence summary in the client's domain language. REQUIRED if any audience entry has id != 'internal'. Voice-scorer evaluates this string against the persona's voice_constitution before ratify.

strata.description: Which 8-strata layer (per domain-ideal-whole-substrate skill) this artifact instantiates. Used by storefront audit to detect strata-coverage gaps per persona.

client_render_mode.description: Hint to the translation engine for output shape. eli5 = collapse jargon, expand metaphors. tldr = preserve structure, compress prose. full = mirror structure, only substitute lexicon. skip = explicitly exclude from storefront even if tagged.

audience.description: One audience target. The substrate produces one _generated/<artifact>.<persona>.client.md per audience entry.

id.description: Persona key. 'internal' means no projection. Otherwise must match a {persona_id}.lexicon.yaml file in ~/Documents/personas/. Examples: rob, maddie, scott.

register.description: Rhetorical mode for this projection (per Product Domain Engine). If absent, defaults to the persona's register_default field.

surface.description: Where this projection deploys. storefront = link-gated subdomain (default). private = auth-required. public = apex/SEO-indexed.

bridge_to.description: Domain handles from the persona's lexicon (e.g. ['chess', 'fitness'] for rob). The substrate uses these to route through the correct lexicon.domains[handle] entry. If absent, all persona domains are eligible.
